ops.body:
Closed in abwc 0.12.1 (cc-skills origin/main 2f3e2ea). C1: bin/ab's create POST no longer uses curl --retry (a lost 5xx could silently re-POST → duplicate); on create failure it adopts an existing tagged session via find_session instead of re-POSTing, so a single invocation creates ≤1 session. C2: `ab close` now reaps ALL tagged RUNNING sessions in a bounded, resumable loop.

NOTE: an initial design also reaped duplicates on the live/establish path — an adversarial gpt-5.6-sol review found that could release the daemon's in-use session (both in the sequential-with-stale-duplicate case and under genuine same-TAG concurrency), so live-path reaping was removed; reaping happens only in close, where nothing is paired. Residual (pre-existing, low, not fixed): a user ~/.curlrc with --retry could still re-inject retry into the create POST — `curl -q` on that call would close it.
